test_plan.strategy: Three layers, all runner-visible from day one (TC id in every test title). UNIT (node --test + vitest components): route parsing, detail-reader whitelist/degradation, validate-runner spawn policy (fixed argv, timeout, cache, dedup), markdown renderer inertness, SummaryTab async states. INTEGRATION (node --test): the two endpoints end-to-end against fixture projects with a stub aitri binary and mocked sockets for the loopback guard — including fs-instrumented hostile-input tests (scope traversal, whitelist) and the spawn census for the process budget. E2E (Playwright, browser page tests in the SAME files/worker family as the suites that share server state): navigation, each tab's happy/edge/negative rendering against seeded fixture projects, XSS inertness probe (window.__pwned). Regression NFRs run the EXISTING suites with unmodified assertions plus id-mapped pins where a new id must appear in runner output. NFR-055 (CI) is enforced by the repository workflow (observable lives in CI; not TC-covered — same disposition as the prior feature). All fixtures under os.tmpdir()/AITRI_HUB_DIR isolation.
test_plan.coverage_goal: every MUST FR and every Regression/Security NFR traced to passing automated tests; renderer and endpoint security paths at 100% branch coverage of their decision points

test_cases.title: card click navigates to the detail route
test_cases.given: hub e2e server on :3099 with registered fixture project 'demo' visible on the overview
test_cases.when: the demo card is clicked in the browser page
test_cases.then: URL becomes /project/<demo-id>; the detail header strip renders the text 'demo'; no full page reload (SPA navigation)
steps: the demo card is clicked in the browser page

test_cases.title: back returns to an intact overview
test_cases.given: browser is on /project/<demo-id> after clicking the card
test_cases.then: URL is '/' and the overview renders the same project cards as before navigation
test_cases.expected_result: history navigation preserved by the useRoute extension

test_cases.title: unknown project id renders not-found
test_cases.given: hub e2e server running
test_cases.then: a not-found state renders with a working link to '/'; zero uncaught console errors; page is not blank

test_cases.title: header strip renders identity fields
test_cases.given: fixture project with aitriVersion '2.0.0-rc.159' and artifactsDir 'spec' collected on the dashboard
test_cases.then: header shows name, status badge, '2.0.0-rc.159' (full tag) and 'spec'; all visible at a 375px-wide viewport without horizontal scroll
test_cases.expected_result: identity always visible while judging evidence

test_cases.title: verdict chip neutral before validate
test_cases.given: detail view opened, validate endpoint NOT yet invoked (Summary verdict panel not opened)
test_cases.then: the verdict chip shows the neutral 'not checked' state — no verdict value is fabricated

test_cases.title: tab switching keeps the header stable
test_cases.given: detail view open on the Test Cases tab
test_cases.when: the user switches to Bugs and back
test_cases.then: the header never unmounts/flickers (same DOM node persists) and no additional detail fetch fires for the unchanged scope
steps: the user switches to Bugs and back

test_cases.title: detail endpoint serves the parsed chain over loopback
test_cases.given: web server started with AITRI_HUB_DIR temp home and a registered fixture project with 01/03/04/05 + BUGS.json
test_cases.then: 200 with detailVersion 1, testCases/traceability/bugs/artifacts sections populated per the fixture

test_cases.title: hostile scope rejected without filesystem access
test_cases.given: running server, fs access instrumented on the reader module
test_cases.then: 400 responses; zero file opens outside the project root recorded by the instrumentation
test_cases.expected_result: scope validation is regex + membership, never a path

test_cases.title: non-loopback request receives 403 before any read
test_cases.given: request handler invoked with a mocked socket whose remoteAddress is 10.0.0.5
test_cases.then: 403 {error:'forbidden'}; the detail reader is never invoked (spy count 0); unknown id over loopback returns 404 JSON

test_cases.title: feature scope renders that feature's own chain
test_cases.given: fixture project with features f1 and f2, each with DISTINCT TC ids in their own 03_TEST_CASES.json
test_cases.then: each response's testCases contains exactly that feature's TC ids; product scope returns the root chain

test_cases.title: featureless project implies product scope
test_cases.given: fixture project with no features/ dir
test_cases.then: 200 product payload with scopes ['product']; the SPA renders no selector for a single-scope project

test_cases.title: unknown feature scope rejected
test_cases.given: fixture project with feature f1 only
test_cases.then: 400 with a JSON error naming the invalid scope; no partial payload

test_cases.title: verdict panel renders the validate contract
test_cases.given: stub 'aitri' on PATH whose validate --json emits a blocked verdict with 2 blocking reasons and 1 advisory
test_cases.when: GET /api/project/<id>/validate, then the SummaryTab renders the payload (component test)
test_cases.then: endpoint returns {available:true, report:<verbatim stub JSON>}; the panel shows the verdict, both reasons and the advisory each with its command; health score and phase list render per the collected record
steps: GET /api/project/<id>/validate, then the SummaryTab renders the payload (component test)
test_cases.expected_result: verdict rendered per VALIDATE_JSON.md, never re-derived

test_cases.title: 60s cache + refresh semantics
test_cases.given: stub aitri counts its invocations to a file
test_cases.when: two GET /validate calls within 60s, then one with refresh=1
test_cases.then: invocation count is 2 total (first call + refresh); the second call served the cache (fetchedAt unchanged)
steps: two GET /validate calls within 60s, then one with refresh=1

test_cases.title: cycle spawns zero validates; CLI absence degrades
test_cases.given: collection cycle run 3 times with the stub counting invocations; then PATH without aitri
test_cases.then: cycle-attributed validate invocations = 0; the no-CLI call returns {available:false, reason} and the panel renders the degraded state (component test)
test_cases.expected_result: process budget intact; no fabricated verdict

test_cases.title: TC table with statuses, counts and manual banner
test_cases.given: fixture scope with 5 TCs: 3 pass, 1 fail, 1 pending manual (with manual_reason)
test_cases.when: the Test Cases tab renders in the browser
test_cases.then: 5 rows with correct statuses; counts strip shows 3 passed / 1 failed / 1 manual-pending; the banner names the pending TC id; the manual row exposes its manual_reason
steps: the Test Cases tab renders in the browser

test_cases.title: filters compose
test_cases.given: same fixture
test_cases.when: filter status=fail applied, then combined with linked FR filter
test_cases.then: exactly the failing TC remains; clearing filters restores 5 rows; a TC with downgraded_from renders its trail
steps: filter status=fail applied, then combined with linked FR filter

test_cases.title: results file absent → pending + guidance
test_cases.given: fixture scope with 03_TEST_CASES.json but no 04_TEST_RESULTS.json
test_cases.then: every TC shows status 'pending' and the state names 'aitri verify-run'; no crash, no blank panel

test_cases.title: uncovered MUST pinned red
test_cases.given: fixture where FR-002 (MUST) has no passing test and FR-001 is covered by 2 TCs
test_cases.then: FR-002 is the first row with a red 'uncovered' marker; FR-001 lists its TCs with latest statuses; priorities visible

test_cases.title: coverage_map + freshness + ac_coverage render
test_cases.given: fixture with coverage_map entries, ac_coverage on FR-001, and a stale requirements-audit stamp
test_cases.then: coverage_map section shows need→disposition; the freshness stamp reads 'stale'; FR-001 shows its ac_coverage; a Hub-derived coverage (no fr_coverage in results) is labeled 'computed by Hub'
test_cases.expected_result: intent coverage visible with honest sourcing

test_cases.title: traceability artifact absent → empty state
test_cases.given: fixture scope without 05_TRACEABILITY.json
test_cases.then: empty state names the traceability artifact and its producing command; no blank panel

test_cases.title: blocking bug pinned with resolution
test_cases.given: fixture BUGS.json with 3 bugs; one critical active with resolution text and linked TC
test_cases.then: the critical bug is pinned first with a red band; severity, status, resolution and linked TC readable; files_changed listed

test_cases.title: absent vs corrupt are distinct states
test_cases.given: two fixtures: no BUGS.json, and BUGS.json '{"bugs":[]}'
test_cases.when: the Bugs tab renders for each
test_cases.then: absent → 'no bugs recorded' state naming 'aitri bug add'; valid-empty → zero-bug table state; the two are visually distinct
steps: the Bugs tab renders for each

test_cases.title: corrupt BUGS.json shows parse error inline
test_cases.given: fixture BUGS.json containing '{invalid'
test_cases.then: inline parse-error state names the scope and says bugs are NOT counted; no empty table presented as zero bugs

test_cases.title: markdown renders formatted; PRD as table
test_cases.given: fixture with 02_SYSTEM_DESIGN.md (headings, list, fenced code) and 01_REQUIREMENTS.json (2 FRs, personas, no_go_zone)
test_cases.then: the design doc shows real <h2>/<ul>/<pre> formatting (not raw text); the PRD shows an FR table with ids, priorities and ACs plus personas and no_go_zone; raw views are collapsed toggles

test_cases.title: chain list marks absences; oversized artifact truncates
test_cases.given: fixture missing 00_DISCOVERY.md and containing a >1MiB markdown artifact
test_cases.then: the chain list marks 00_DISCOVERY.md as not produced (optional phase); the oversized artifact renders its first 1 MiB with a 'truncated' notice

test_cases.title: script content renders inert
test_cases.given: fixture markdown artifact containing '<script>window.__pwned=1</script>' and an onclick attribute
test_cases.then: the script text appears as escaped inert text; window.__pwned is undefined; no dialog/handler fires

test_cases.title: every tab has a guided empty state
test_cases.given: a bare fixture project (only .aitri, no artifacts)
test_cases.when: the detail payload is fetched and each of the 5 tabs renders (component tests)
test_cases.then: each tab renders a non-empty state naming its artifact and the producing aitri command (5 assertions)
steps: the detail payload is fetched and each of the 5 tabs renders (component tests)

test_cases.title: snapshot-degraded project shows the banner
test_cases.given: fixture whose collected record carries degradationReason 'version_too_old'
test_cases.then: the degradation banner shows with the required CLI version; tabs still render from the fallback data

test_cases.title: malformed artifact degrades that section only
test_cases.given: fixture with malformed 03_TEST_CASES.json but valid BUGS.json
test_cases.when: the detail payload is built and tabs render
test_cases.then: testCases section is available:false with the file named; Bugs tab renders normally; zero uncaught exceptions
steps: the detail payload is built and tabs render

test_cases.title: overview renders unchanged with the feature present
test_cases.given: existing overview e2e fixtures
test_cases.when: the pre-existing overview/snapshot-card e2e suites run
test_cases.then: all pass with UNMODIFIED assertions
steps: the pre-existing overview/snapshot-card e2e suites run

test_cases.title: dashboard.json schema untouched
test_cases.given: an unchanged fixture collected before and after the feature code
test_cases.then: empty diff — no field added/removed/retyped by this feature in dashboard.json

test_cases.title: route extension does not break '/' and '/admin'
test_cases.given: useRoute unit fixtures for '/', '/admin', '/project/x', '/unknown'
test_cases.then: '/' → overview, '/admin' → admin, '/project/x' → detail with id 'x', '/unknown' → overview fallback exactly as before the change

test_cases.title: cycle spawn census unchanged
test_cases.given: stub aitri counting invocations per argv; 2 eligible fixture projects
test_cases.then: exactly 3×2 status --json invocations and 0 validate invocations attributed to the cycle
test_cases.expected_result: one spawn per eligible project per cycle, nothing else

test_cases.title: open detail view adds no cycle processes
test_cases.given: same census with a detail view payload having been served (validate cached)
test_cases.then: validate invocation count does not increase during cycles

test_cases.title: validate endpoint cannot be amplified
test_cases.given: 10 concurrent GET /validate requests fired at once
test_cases.then: exactly 1 aitri validate spawn occurs; all 10 responses carry the same fetchedAt
test_cases.expected_result: dedup bounds the worst-case click storm

test_cases.title: validate spawn is fixed-argv execFile
test_cases.given: spawn layer instrumented
test_cases.when: runValidate is invoked for a project whose id/name contain shell metacharacters ('; rm -rf', backticks)
test_cases.then: the spawn is execFile('aitri',['validate','--json']) with cwd set — argv contains NO project-derived strings; no shell involved
steps: runValidate is invoked for a project whose id/name contain shell metacharacters ('; rm -rf', backticks)

test_cases.title: artifact whitelist enforced
test_cases.given: detail-reader fs instrumentation
test_cases.when: readDetail runs on a fixture that also contains 'secrets.env' and '.git/config'
test_cases.then: only whitelisted artifact filenames are opened; the extra files are never read and never appear in the payload
steps: readDetail runs on a fixture that also contains 'secrets.env' and '.git/config'

test_cases.title: dangerouslySetInnerHTML absent
test_cases.given: the feature's web source files
test_cases.when: a lint-style scan runs over web/src for dangerouslySetInnerHTML
test_cases.then: zero occurrences in the feature's components (pinned so ADR-Q2's invariant survives refactors)
steps: a lint-style scan runs over web/src for dangerouslySetInnerHTML

test_cases.title: admin CRUD suite unchanged
test_cases.given: existing admin-api e2e suite (incl. TC-142h/e wrappers)
test_cases.when: the suite runs with the new routes registered
test_cases.then: all pass with unmodified assertions
steps: the suite runs with the new routes registered

test_cases.title: new routes do not shadow admin routes
test_cases.given: running server
test_cases.when: GET /api/projects and GET /api/project/<id>/detail are both dispatched
test_cases.then: each hits its own handler (admin list unchanged; detail served) — no route-prefix collision
steps: GET /api/projects and GET /api/project/<id>/detail are both dispatched

test_cases.title: malformed detail URLs never reach admin handlers
test_cases.given: running server
test_cases.then: 400/404 JSON from the detail validation — admin handlers untouched (spy), no crash

test_cases.title: detail endpoint under 500ms on the 200-TC fixture
test_cases.given: generated fixture: 200 TCs, 10 features
test_cases.then: median response ≤500ms on CI hardware

test_cases.title: summary renders before validate resolves
test_cases.given: SummaryTab mounted with a validate promise that resolves after 2s (fake timers)
test_cases.then: health score, phases and feature table are visible immediately; the verdict panel shows a loading state until resolution
test_cases.expected_result: async verdict never blocks the tab

test_cases.title: validate timeout degrades cleanly
test_cases.given: runValidate with a stub that never exits (killed at the 30s timeout, fake-timed)
test_cases.then: result is {available:false, reason} mentioning timeout; the process is killed; no unhandled rejection
test_cases.expected_result: hung CLI cannot hang the Hub
